log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, signed, and has persistence capabilities. It has been analyzed in multiple sandboxes, which can provide more information about its behavior.

The sample does not appear to have any network activity, but it has dropped 10 files and created 10 processes. It also has 5 registry persistence keys. The highlighted API call is SetWindowsHookExA, w

The sample has a wide range of capabilities, including file and registry operations, process manipulation, and system information gathering. The highlighted behaviors include creating or opening files

The sample creates multiple processes, including v4barsvc.exe, v4brmon.exe, v4highin.exe, and v4setup.pe32. It also loads several modules, including npv4stub.dll, t8res.dll, and v4brstub.dll. The high
The CAPA sandbox has identified 13 MITRE ATT&CK techniques, but the exact techniques are not specified. The sample also matches 32 signatures, but the nature of these signatures is not clear.

The sample does not appear to have any network activity, including IP traffic, DNS lookups, or HTTP conversations. This suggests that the sample may be a local-only malware or may be using a different

The sample has created 5 registry persistence keys, including entries in the Run key for DictionaryBoss Search Scope Monitor and DictionaryBoss Browser Plugin Loader. These keys are used to ensure the
The sample has dropped 10 files, including DLLs and executables, to various locations on the system. These files are likely used for persistence and to carry out malicious activities.
